name: 02-zoom-anchor-whitespace
description: Regression for 'zoom snaps page sideways when cursor is on white margin'. At scale 1.0 the A4 page is narrower than the container — white margins on both sides. Ctrl+wheel zoom-in with the cursor parked on the RIGHT white margin must NOT shift scrollLeft, because the anchor block is intentionally skipped when the cursor is off-page. Uses probe-relative coordinates so the test works regardless of side-panel state.

assertions.label: scrollLeft drift < 100 px on white-margin zoom (original snap-to-fill bug shifted hundreds of px; reflow can drift up to ~scale_change * scrollLeft)
scale actually increased (wheel handler did fire)
